author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
Sequencing note: this ticket sits between `199ed91`
(Bug → Issue rename, lands first) and `69d5e1b`
(`agent-ready`, lands third, blocked on this).

If `199ed91` ships first, this ticket lands against
the renamed `IssueRecord` / `IssueId` types. If it
ships in either order, fold the spec bumps into a
single v2 cut.

Dep edge from `69d5e1b` recorded in comments only —
the CLI doesn't expose `add_dependency` yet (storage
has the primitive). When CLI dep verbs land, the
edge becomes machine-readable.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
**Findings.** v2.1 schema landed end-to-end: `IssueType` enum (bug/feature/epic/research/roadmap/unspecified, serde-lowercase, default Unspecified) and `slug: Option<String>` (kebab-case `[a-z0-9-]+`, 3–48 chars, no leading/trailing/consecutive hyphens) are first-class on `IssueRecord`. Trailers carry `Op::SetType` and `Op::SetSlug`. `Storage::resolve(handle)` accepts either a 7-hex id or a slug, walking `list_ids` for the slug case; slug uniqueness is enforced across open issues at write time. Every id-taking CLI verb (`show`, `update`, `comment`, `close`, `open`, `label add|rm`) routes through `resolve`. Filters: `jjf ls --type <kind>` (repeatable, OR), `jjf ls --slug <substring>`. Spec bumped to v2.1 in `docs/storage-format.md` (§3 fields, §3.x slug rules, §5.2 op vocab, §5.7 multi-op create); error kinds `invalid_slug` / `slug_collision` / `slug_not_found` documented in `docs/cli-json.md`. Backfill applied to all 10 existing tickets in this commit's session — `jjf show agent-ready` resolves; `jjf ls --type epic` returns exactly 6 rows.

**Recommendation.** Close. The schema, CLI, docs, migration, and the 212-test workspace are all green and self-consistent. `69d5e1b` (`agent-ready`) is unblocked and can sort by the new type field.

**Confidence.** High. Tests cover record round-trip after v1→v2 migration, slug validation, resolver, type+slug filter, and multi-op create. The migration recipe ran cleanly on the live planner.

**Open follow-ups.** None for this ticket. Adjacent work, filed separately: `jjf ready` priority sort against the new types (`69d5e1b`, next up); persistent slug index if read-all becomes hot; reserved-slug deny-list if collisions surface; slug history surfacing in `show`.
